content.full_text:
Fnrn PolrrrcAl PnecrrcBs CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
June 15,2010
Rick Ehrenfeld
220 LOth Street
Del Mar, CA920l4
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. .{-10-089
Dear Mr. Ehrenfeld:
This letter responds to yollr request for funher advice regarding conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act").1
This supplements our letter of April 2l,2OlO, (Ehrenfetd Advice Letter, No. A-10-062).
This letter is based on additional information you provided in your letter of May 6, z1l1,and in
our telephone conversation on June 3, 2010. The facts in your prior request for advice are
incorporated herein by reference, except for those portions inconsisteni with the new facts or
clarifications provided.
Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that
may have already taken place, and any conclusions in this letter apply only to prospective
actions. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The f'air poiiti.al practices
Commission ("the Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (7,r
re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We
therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as
common law conflict of interest or Govemment code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
May you make, participate in making, or influence a governmental decision regarding a
change in zoning, from traditional zoning to form-based zoning, for the City of Del Mar's
commercial district despite the fact that your personal residence, which yo, o*n, is in or within
500 feet of the district?
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government code Sections 81000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ret'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the p'iir political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title z of tire Califbrnia Code of Regulations. ^All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
"fi;'


File No. A-10-089
Page No.2
CONCLUSION
Based on the additional facts provided and the general nature of the decision at this time,
we conclude that you qualify under the public generally exception in Regulation 1g707.1, as
discussed below- Therefore, you may make, participate in making, or inlluence governmental
decisions regarding a change in zoning, from traditional zoning to form-based zoning, for the
City of Del Mar's commercial district. However, please note that the public generally exception
may not apply to more specific zoning decisions that come before your ug"nJ in the future,
FACTS
In your May 6, 2010 request for further advice and in a telephone conversation on June 3,
2010 you submitted additional information to supplement your original request for advice.
(Ehrenfeld Advice Letter, supra.)
You are planning commissioner for the City of Del Mar and are seeking advice regarding
your participation in-a public hearing regarding a change in zoning to convert iire city,s cinent -
commercial district from traditional zoning to form-based zoning, *hich would permit the city to
set parameters for development within the district on a lot by lot basis.
Your primary residence, of which you are the sole owner, is outside the commercial
district. It is outside the form-based zoning implementation area but is within 500 feet of it. you
wish to know whether you qualify under the public generally exception and whether you may
nonetheless participate in decisions involving form-based zoning dispite a conflict-of-interest.
You state in your request for further advice that the estimates for the total number of
residences in Del Mar range from 1750 to 2178. The 2000 Census states the total number of
residences in Del Mar is 2178. You used the city's geographical information system to create a
map showing a 500 foot radius surrounding the intend"d r"rore area. you also compileJ a lisrof
275 residences that fall within the 500 foot radius of the FBC implementation area. you state
that these residences are comparable to your home in lot size, home size and will be affected in
substantially the same manner as your home by the proposed zoning change. In addition, you
state that lots in Del Mar are relatively uniform in size. Allowable home sizes in Del Mar are
based on a floor area ratio and residences on similarly sized lots are of similar size.
Because your question deals solely with the application of the public generally exception
in Regulation 18707.1, we limit our analysis to this rirrgt" issue.
ANALYSIS
Public Generally Exception: The General Rule
Even if a public official determines that his or her economic interests will experience a
material financial effect as a result of the govemmental decision before the official. he or she


File No. A-10-089
Page No. 3
may still participate under the "public generally" exception if the material financial effect of a
governmental decision on a public official's economic interests is indistinguishable from its
effect on the public generally. (Section 87103; Regulation lg7o7.)
Under the basic public generally ntle, you must meet a two-part test by showing that the
decision would affect a "significant segment" of the public in "substantial the same manner,, as it
financially affects your economic interest.
For decisions that affect a public official's real property, "significant segment,' is defined
as lO-percent or more of all property owners or all ,"tiaeittoi property ownrri inDel Mar, or
5,000 property owners or residential property owners in the city. Youinclude information about
the total number of properties in Del Mar (2,178) and state naiZlS residences similar in size and
lot size to your property are within 500 feet of the commercial district that is slated for rezoning.
While an official must indentify a significant segment consisting of ten percent or more
residential property owners or 5,000 residenlial propert; owners, Regulation
18707.1(b)(lxB)(iii) permits an official "to count eachiesidential priperry affected as being
owned by one property owner if, and only if, the official counts himseif oiherself as the sole
owner of the public official's residential property regardless of his or her actual ownership
interest."
Based upon the number of residential properties you have identified that are in or within
500 feet of the FBC rezone area, it appears that dicisioni regarding the project area will affect
the minimum threshold of ten percent of the property owners as provided in Regulation
18707.1(b)(lXBXi), so long as you choose to count each resideriiul prop"rty aflected as being
owned by one property owner as specified in Regularion(bXl)(B)(iii).
In the next step, you must also determine the financial effect on your economic interests
and measure this effect against the financial effect on the public generaliy. The minimum
threshold of the significant segment identified must be affected ii "substintially the same
manner" as your economic interest.
Many factors play a role in determining whether or not an economic interest in real
property will be financially affected in a manner substantially the same as the significant
segment identified including, but not limited to: the proximity of the property to a specific
project or property subject to the government decision; the location oi tn! prlperty;-the impacr
on the property from noise or traffic; the impact on the property due to aeu"topment potential;
and the size of a property . (see Regulation 18707.1(bXlXA); olro ,". Bergei Advici Letrer, No.
A-os-054.)
Based on additional facts you provided, it appears that the significant segment will be
affected in substantially the same manner as your property interests due to its priximity to the
project area, similar lot size and home sizes.


File No. A-10-089
Page No.4
Therefore, we conclude based on your facts and the general nature of the decision at this
time, that you qualify under the public generally exception in Regulation 18707.1. Therefore,
you may make, participate in making, or influence a governmental decision regarding a change
in zoning, from traditional zoning to form-based zoning, for the City of Del Mir's commercial
district.
However, please note that the public generally exception may not apply to more specific
zoning decisions that come before your agency in the futurJ. Depending onifrl nature of the
decision, your personal residence may not be affected in substantially the same manner as the
significant segment. Factors that affect this determination include the specific type of decision
involved, the proximity of the subject property to your residence, the impact of the decision on
the surrounding area (such as noise and traffic) and impacts on the prop"rty', development
potential.
Should you have questions about future zoning decisions, please contact us for further
advice at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By:
Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
ERjgl
